message:
fix(search): canonicalize and deduplicate search results, harden indexing

Three interconnected correctness issues addressed:

1. **Search result canonicalization** (search_service.rs): The lexical,
   semantic, and hybrid search paths each had inline hydration logic that
   only filled in missing titles — it never enforced query filters
   (project, product, agent, thread, importance, time range, ack) on
   results returned by Tantivy/semantic backends. This meant lexical and
   semantic engines could return results outside the caller's scope
   (wrong project, wrong agent, etc.) because those filters only existed
   in the SQL planner path. Extracted a unified `canonicalize_message_results`
   pipeline that deduplicates by id, fetches authoritative row data, and
   applies all query filters post-hoc. All three engine paths now call it.

2. **Upsert-based indexing** (search_v3.rs): `add_indexable_message` was
   append-only — re-indexing an edited or redelivered message produced
   duplicate documents with the same id, corrupting search ranking and
   counts. Introduced `upsert_indexable_message` which deletes-then-adds
   by Term on the id field, guaranteeing exactly one live document per
   message id. Both single and batch index paths now use upsert. Also
   replaced `#[allow(clippy::cast_sign_loss)]` with explicit
   `u64::try_from` to reject negative ids at index time rather than
   silently wrapping.

3. **Deterministic backfill** (search_v3.rs): The watermark-based skip
   logic (`existing_count >= db_count`) was fragile — it silently skipped
   backfill when the index had stale docs from a previous DB, and the
   zero-count guard added complexity without full coverage. Replaced with
   a clean `delete_all_documents` + full rebuild strategy, which is
   correct by construction and eliminates an entire class of stale-index
   bugs after DB resets, migrations, or interrupted runs.

4. **Robot project resolution** (robot.rs): Changed `LIKE` to exact
   `lower()=lower()` match for slug lookup and plain `=` for human_key
   to prevent wildcard injection (e.g. `%` in user input matching
   unintended projects). Added `trim()` on input. Removed stale doc
   comments about LIKE parity.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
